title: The attraction to 14 year old girls
display_title: The attraction to 14 year old girls
wikitext:
[]: The attraction to 14 year old girls..             Quote Post by ericm » Tue Jun 07, 2011 11:27 pm I am wondering peoples' opinions here as far as the realization that many 'fanboys' are attracted to the 14 year old girls of Eva..I personally have never had much of an opinion on either fanboys in general, nor the large attraction to these characters who are 14. Is anyone familiar with the standards in Japan as far as dating/attraction goes? Age of consent? I am ignorant of these things. Anyone know how Anno's apparently strange attitude towards sexuality fits into this discussion? If any feel this is a conversation better suited for one of the Eva sections of this forum, and I was tempted to post it there rather than here, feel free to move it.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Jun 07, 2011 11:37 pm The kids in Eva are drawn basically like slightly shorter adults. This is pretty much all the explanation needed for the amount of fanbase attraction to them.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Tue Jun 07, 2011 11:56 pm Yeah. The girls in Eva really don't look like they're 13-15, they look like they're 18-20. That's pretty much all the explanation you need. Well, that and Japan as a culture has an intense fixation on youth, and as a society much less stringent definitions of statutory rape, child porn, etc.

[]:             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Wed Jun 08, 2011 12:02 am I'm not going to try and hide behind the "they're drawn like shorter adults" excuse. Asuka has the body of a 14-year-old just as much as she has the body of an 18-year-old (mainly because her figure in the TV series + EoE is about as consistent as a bi-polar chimpanzee), in Rebuild she mostly sticks to body type that fits her age. That said, it's a fictional character, and Asuka's personality/character is the reason she's my waifu, not her looks.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jun 08, 2011 1:00 am On the one hand... Who can tell me what this is? W x Y When drawn in their plugsuits, the implied geometries say "nubile female"; historically recent trends of increasingly early menarche and extended childhood don't play well with how human psychology and physiology developed. "In the wild" the earlier onset of obvious female physical maturity seems to have been to hasten the transition from "useless mouth" to "available mate" (while boys held back on the transition from "future asset" to "rival"). On the other... Have you looked at other anime girls, though? Even notionally nine year old girls get drawn with mature figures ( albeit often with candy-apple heads ); it's the characters who are close to the end of their highschool careers that get drawn flat-chested or generally infantile in proportions .

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Jun 08, 2011 2:16 am Pretty much what everybody else has said. It's not the fact that they're fourteen that attracts people to them; it's that they look like matured adults that just happen to be fourteen. :P

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Wed Jun 08, 2011 2:44 am The real metric is how far you take the abstract. Seeing a representation of the female form and having your genetic hindbrain instruct you to hit that like the fist of an angry god is different from spending your inheritance to memorialize that particular representation of the female form in ceramic. Over-indulging in the abstract should be as socially acceptable as eating yourself obese. ...possibly I've strayed from topic.

[]:             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Wed Jun 08, 2011 3:44 am I first watched Evangelion when I was fifteen, I think. I found Asuka and Rei pretty damn attractive back then, and I find it a little hard to detach myself from this view just because I passed an arbitrary age limit.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Wed Jun 08, 2011 4:49 am First of all, I have to say that as a 19-year old I still find girls 13 and up to be at least somewhat sexually attractive, sometimes even moreso than older women because I find that (at least when it comes to white people) girls already pretty much look like women when they've reached the age of 14-15 or so, except that they#re often cuter. Don't get me wrong, I find ''older'' (as in 20s) women still immensely attractive, but to say that 13-year olds can't be sexy is just dishonest IMO. When it comes to Eva, I don't think they really exaggerated the character's figures all that much, especially Asuka's since she's supposed to be European. And anyway do find that Rei (who I'm guessing is supposed to be Asian) does look younger than Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Wed Jun 08, 2011 5:34 am Are real 14 year old girls attractive enough for 14 year old boys? There´s your answer.

[]:             Quote Post by AngelNo13Bardiel » Wed Jun 08, 2011 5:53 am As has been stated a few times above me: physically look their age or slightly over, definitely older than that mentally. And honestly, for me anyway, cute > hot. LeoXiao wrote: First of all, I have to say that as a 19-year old I still find girls 13 and up to be at least somewhat sexually attractive, sometimes even moreso than older women because I find that (at least when it comes to white people) girls already pretty much look like women when they've reached the age of 14-15 or so, except that they#re often cuter. Don't get me wrong, I find ''older'' (as in 20s) women still immensely attractive, but to say that 13-year olds can't be sexy is just dishonest IMO.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Wed Jun 08, 2011 12:14 pm Huh, when I was fourteen, the female population at my school weren't nearly as attractive as what I saw in Evangelion. Where are these hot fourteen year olds everyone keeps saying exist in the real world?

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Wed Jun 08, 2011 12:35 pm 12-15 is the time when human girls are the most fertile.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Wed Jun 08, 2011 12:45 pm Trajan wrote: Where are these hot fourteen year olds everyone keeps saying exist in the real world?

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Wed Jun 08, 2011 12:51 pm How attraction works: http://www.hulu.com/watch/210121/archer-the-perfect-gentleman#x-4,vclip,1,0 0:25-0:40 XD

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Wed Jun 08, 2011 1:28 pm TehDonutKing wrote: 12-15 is the time when human girls are the most fertile.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Wed Jun 08, 2011 1:30 pm Trajan wrote: Huh, when I was fourteen, the female population at my school weren't nearly as attractive as what I saw in Evangelion. Where are these hot fourteen year olds everyone keeps saying exist in the real world?

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Wed Jun 08, 2011 1:49 pm Who can tell me what this is? W x Y

[]:             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Wed Jun 08, 2011 2:05 pm I think we really should be less worried about the pedophilia aspect of people being attracted to the Evangelion girls (sans Misato and the rest of the "of age" female cast) , and instead be more worried about people being attracted to extreme basket cases/girls who most people wouldn't touch with a 15 foot pole for fear of being crushed under the emotional baggage.

[]:             Quote Post by AngelNo13Bardiel » Wed Jun 08, 2011 3:04 pm ^ Yeah, that issue is one I can see the point of asking "why". In my case...I'd say it's because I've honestly dealt with more than my share (and I'm sure some of you guys' shares, too) of basket cases/emotional baggage/other similar shit from girls I've known over the years. It's just like anything else: if you've been around it long enough, you'll build up a tolerance. For the most part, it's just about trying to understand their mindset through trial-and-error (key word: error).
